public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), will DRC report at least 1 NEW affected health zone beyond the 36 already affected as of the frozen baseline (WHO DON612, data 2026-07-01: Ituri 24, North Kivu 11, South Kivu 1 = 36 national affected health zones)? Resolves on public evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05. This is a relative-high spatial-extent forecast: the geography model ranks continued in-country expansion as more likely than not on an active Ituri-anchored plateau, but the ranking is NOT a calibrated diffusion probability.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: YES if any WHO DON/SitRep, ECDC CDTR, or INSP/DRC MoH SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05 reports a count of DRC affected health zones >= 37, OR names a specific health zone as newly affected/confirmed that is not among the 36 in the baseline. NO if the affected-health-zone total remains at 36 or lower through 2026-08-04. Frozen baseline = 36 national affected health zones (Ituri 24 / North Kivu 11 / South Kivu 1). Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), will the karisimbi (North Kivu, DRC) health zone reach its FIRST lab-confirmed BDBV case dated on or after 2026-07-05? Resolves ONLY on a first lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05, so if karisimbi was already affected before that date this pin resolves NO. This is a relative-mid corridor-watch forecast reflecting a mid-ranked North Kivu edge, NOT a calibrated arrival probability.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: YES only if a WHO DON/SitRep, ECDC CDTR, or INSP/DRC MoH SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05 records the FIRST laboratory-confirmed BDBV case in karisimbi health zone with that first confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05. NO if karisimbi records no first confirmation in-window, OR if its first confirmation predates 2026-07-05 (already-affected zones resolve NO). Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), will the mahagi (Ituri, DRC) health zone, our named #1 corridor blindspot on the Mahagi/Goli-Arua axis, reach its FIRST lab-confirmed BDBV case dated on or after 2026-07-05? resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.

public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), CONDITIONAL on at least one of {karisimbi, mahagi} AND kisangani (Tshopo, DRC) both reaching a first BDBV lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05, will a nearer Ituri/North-Kivu edge zone (karisimbi OR mahagi) reach that first confirmation on a date at or before kisangani's first confirmation? Resolves ONLY on first confirmations dated on or after 2026-07-05. This is a relative-high pairwise ordinal forecast: the geography model ranks the nearer edge ahead of the farther Tshopo capital, and ordinal rank is the calibrated-ranking claim (plain-distance within-anchor AUC ~0.68), NOT a diffusion probability.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Scoreable ONLY if BOTH a nearer edge (karisimbi or mahagi) AND kisangani each register a FIRST lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05 within the window. YES if the earliest qualifying nearer-edge first-confirmation date is <= kisangani's first-confirmation date. NO if kisangani confirms strictly before every qualifying nearer-edge zone. If the antecedent is not met (kisangani never confirms in-window, or no nearer edge confirms in-window), the pairwise ordinal is not evaluable and resolves per the ledger's not-evaluable handling, not YES/NO. Any confirmation predating 2026-07-05 does not count. Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), will at least 1 of the four named far zones {lubero (North Kivu), kalehe (South Kivu), kabare (South Kivu), bafwasende (Tshopo)} reach its FIRST BDBV lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05? Resolves ONLY on a first lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05, so any of these four already affected before that date does not count. resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: YES if any of lubero, kalehe, kabare, or bafwasende records a FIRST laboratory-confirmed BDBV case, dated on or after 2026-07-05, in a WHO DON/SitRep, ECDC CDTR, or INSP/DRC MoH SitRep. NO if none of the four records a first confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05 through 2026-08-04. A first confirmation predating 2026-07-05 for any of these zones does not count toward YES. Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), for at least one health zone not among the 36 affected at baseline, will that zone appear in public suspected/alert/probable reporting (dated on or after 2026-07-05) BEFORE its own first lab-confirmation appears (also dated on or after 2026-07-05)? Resolves ONLY on public evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05. This is a relative-mid detection-lead forecast: we expect at least one new zone to surface first as suspected/alert before it confirms, testing whether surveillance leads confirmation.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: YES if, for at least one zone outside the baseline 36, a WHO/ECDC/INSP source dated on or after 2026-07-05 lists it as suspected/alert/probable on a date strictly earlier than the date it first appears as laboratory-confirmed (that confirmation also dated on or after 2026-07-05). NO if every new zone that confirms in-window appears as confirmed on the same date as (or earlier than) any suspected/alert listing, OR if no new zone surfaces in either state in-window. Zones already affected before 2026-07-05 do not count. Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), across the set of health zones that reach a first BDBV lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05 AND that also appear in public suspected/alert reporting dated on or after 2026-07-05, will the public detected-order (first suspected/alert date) rank be consistent with (>=) the confirmed-order (first confirmation date) rank, i.e. zones detected earlier as suspected also confirm earlier? Resolves ONLY on public evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05. This is a relative-mid rank-consistency forecast, testing monotone agreement between detection order and confirmation order, NOT a calibrated probability.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Scoreable only if at least 2 new zones each have BOTH a first suspected/alert date and a first confirmation date, all dated on or after 2026-07-05, in WHO/ECDC/INSP public reporting. YES if the ordering of zones by first suspected/alert date matches (is non-inverted relative to) their ordering by first confirmation date for the qualifying pairs (detected-order rank >= confirmed-order rank, no rank inversion). NO if any qualifying pair is inverted (a zone suspected later nonetheless confirms earlier than a zone suspected earlier). If fewer than 2 qualifying zones exist, the pin is not evaluable and resolves per the ledger's not-evaluable handling. Dates predating 2026-07-05 do not count. Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), CONDITIONAL on at least two NEW Uganda districts (beyond the baseline Kampala and Wakiso) each reaching a first BDBV lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05, will the district geographically nearer to the Kampala/Wakiso cluster reach its first confirmation on a date at or before the farther district's first confirmation? Resolves ONLY on first confirmations dated on or after 2026-07-05. This is a relative-mid pairwise ordinal forecast for onward Ugandan spread; ordinal rank (nearer-before-farther) is the ranking claim, NOT a calibrated diffusion probability.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Scoreable ONLY if at least 2 NEW Uganda districts (excluding Kampala and Wakiso) each register a FIRST lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05 within the window, per Uganda MoH, WHO DON/SitRep, or ECDC CDTR. Among the earliest-confirming pair, YES if the district nearer (by road/centroid distance) to the Kampala/Wakiso cluster confirms on a date <= the farther district's first-confirmation date; NO if the farther district confirms strictly first. If fewer than 2 new districts confirm in-window, the pairwise ordinal is not evaluable and resolves per the ledger's not-evaluable handling, not YES/NO. Baseline districts = Kampala, Wakiso (already affected; excluded). Confirmations predating 2026-07-05 do not count. Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: HOLD forecast (high probability): Bundibugyo district, Uganda records NO locally-acquired (in-district) confirmed BDBV case with onset or confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05 during the 30-day window ending 2026-08-04. As of the 2026-07-05 baseline, Uganda's only affected districts are Kampala and Wakiso; Bundibugyo is unaffected. This pin holds if that remains true. Bundibugyo is a first-order land-border district facing Ituri/North Kivu and is the highest-salience Ugandan cross-border watch cell; a hold is the forecast, not the null.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.

An already-listed district would NOT trigger; Bundibugyo is not listed as of baseline. YES (hold confirmed) if no such confirmation appears by 2026-08-04. Lower bound for any triggering evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: TRIPWIRE forecast: does ANY outside-DRC district that is NOT already affected as of 2026-07-05 record its first locally-acquired (community-transmission or in-district secondary) confirmed BDBV case with confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05, within the 30-day window ending 2026-08-04? The frozen affected-district set for Uganda at baseline is {Kampala, Wakiso}; France (1 imported case, no onward transmission) and the United States (imported) have no locally-acquired transmission. This tripwire records whether cross-border/international transmission converts into a first locally-acquired case in a genuinely NEW district anywhere outside DRC. The forward forecast leans toward NO (zero new conversions).
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolves YES (tripwire fires) if WHO DON/IHR, WHO AFRO, ECDC, or the relevant national health authority confirms a first locally-acquired BDBV case in any district outside DRC that is not in {Kampala, Wakiso} and was not already listed as affected at 2026-07-05, with confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05. Imported/travel-associated cases with no onward local transmission do NOT fire the tripwire. Resolves NO if no such conversion is confirmed by 2026-08-04. Lower bound for triggering evidence: 2026-07-05.

public_question: MAGNITUDE forecast: does Uganda's cumulative national confirmed BDBV case count reach 30 or more at any point during the 30-day window ending 2026-08-04? The frozen baseline at 2026-07-05 is 20 confirmed cases (per WHO DON612 / ECDC). This is a forward magnitude threshold on the national confirmed total, not a per-district or corridor pin. The forecast leans toward NOT reaching 30 (i.e. the outbreak stays below a +10 confirmed escalation over the window).
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolves YES if any WHO DON/IHR update, WHO AFRO situation report, or Uganda MoH report dated on or after 2026-07-05 states a cumulative Uganda confirmed BDBV count of >=30. Resolves NO if the highest reported cumulative confirmed count through 2026-08-04 remains <=29. Uses cumulative confirmed only (not suspected/probable). Baseline 20 frozen at 2026-07-05.

public_question: RATE forecast: does Uganda add at most 2 NEW cumulative confirmed BDBV cases over the 30-day window (i.e. cumulative national confirmed rises from the frozen baseline of 20 at 2026-07-05 to no more than 22 by 2026-08-04)? This is a forward rate/increment commitment measuring new confirmed cases accrued after registration, distinct from the magnitude-threshold pin P4f. The forecast is that transmission stays nearly flat (<=2 new confirmed).
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolves YES if the highest cumulative Uganda confirmed count reported in any WHO DON/IHR, WHO AFRO, or Uganda MoH source dated on or after 2026-07-05 and through 2026-08-04 is <=22 (i.e. at most +2 over baseline 20). Resolves NO if cumulative confirmed reaches >=23 at any point in the window. New confirmed = reported cumulative minus baseline 20. Baseline frozen at 2026-07-05.

public_question: RATE forecast: does Uganda's locally-acquired (secondary / non-imported) share of cumulative confirmed BDBV cases stay at or below 40% through the 30-day window ending 2026-08-04? The frozen baseline at 2026-07-05 is 5 secondary cases (contacts and health workers) out of 20 confirmed = 25% local-acquired share. This forward pin forecasts that in-country transmission does NOT intensify enough to push the locally-acquired fraction above 40%. It is a share/rate commitment, not a raw count or a corridor pin.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolves YES if, in the latest WHO DON/IHR, WHO AFRO, or Uganda MoH breakdown dated on or after 2026-07-05 and through 2026-08-04 that reports both cumulative confirmed and the secondary/locally-acquired (non-imported) count, the locally-acquired share is <=40%. Resolves NO if that share exceeds 40% in any such breakdown within the window. If no source in the window reports the split, resolution falls back to the last in-window reported split; the pin is NOT declared unscoreable. Baseline 5 secondary of 20 confirmed (25%) frozen at 2026-07-05.

public_question: Will Kampala district's cumulative confirmed BDBV count rise above the FIRST Kampala-district figure that WHO or the Uganda Ministry of Health publishes on or after 2026-07-05? No public Kampala-district split exists at registration (Uganda national = 20 confirmed across Kampala and Wakiso per WHO DON612 data 2026-07-01), so this self-anchors to the first published on/after-2026-07-05 district figure rather than a fabricated baseline; if no Kampala-district figure is published in-window it resolves NO.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: YES if a later WHO/Uganda-MoH Kampala-district cumulative confirmed count (dated on/after 2026-07-05) exceeds the FIRST such on/after-2026-07-05 published Kampala-district count. NO if no Kampala-district figure is published in-window, or the district count does not rise above its first published value through 2026-08-04. No fabricated baseline; self-anchored. Lower bound: 2026-07-05.

This is a forward extension pin, not a 'will onward transmission occur' pin (onward transmission is already established). Baseline set frozen 2026-07-05.

public_question: Will point-of-entry (PoE) exit/entry screening at Entebbe International Airport and Kampala-area points of entry remain operational at or above its current posture (screening in place, not suspended or scaled back), per Uganda Ministry of Health / WHO / IOM public reporting dated on or after 2026-07-05? notes: Resolves YES (hold-or-rise) if any Uganda MoH, WHO, or IOM public report dated on/after 2026-07-05 confirms PoE screening at Entebbe/Kampala points of entry remains active or is expanded. Resolves NO only if a public source dated on/after 2026-07-05 reports that PoE screening was suspended, discontinued, or materially scaled back. Absence of any contrary public report through 2026-08-04, with the last-known posture being active screening, resolves YES (posture holds). Baseline frozen 2026-07-05.

public_question: Will published border-management postures at DRC-Uganda border crossings hold status (no border closure, no new formal cross-border movement restriction, and no lifting of existing screening) relative to the 2026-07-05 posture, per WHO / IOM / national authority public reporting dated on or after 2026-07-05? The frozen 2026-07-05 posture is: borders open with cross-border screening at designated points of entry, no closures declared.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolves YES (status holds) if no WHO, IOM, or national-authority public source dated on/after 2026-07-05 reports a change to DRC-Uganda border posture (i.e. no border closure, no newly imposed cross-border movement restriction, and existing screening not withdrawn). Resolves NO if any such source dated on/after 2026-07-05 reports a border closure, a new formal movement restriction, or withdrawal of cross-border screening at DRC-Uganda crossings, through 2026-08-04. Baseline frozen 2026-07-05.

public_question: Will the cumulative confirmed BDBV death toll for the DRC (all affected health zones combined) reach 700 or more, as reported in a WHO Disease Outbreak News, WHO AFRO Ebola/Sudan-virus situation report, or Africa CDC update dated on or after 2026-07-05? Frozen registration baseline is 452 confirmed deaths (WHO DON612, 2026-07-01). Resolves YES only if a governing public source dated on/after 2026-07-05 reports a cumulative DRC confirmed-death figure of at least 700.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Source: WHO Disease Outbreak News / WHO AFRO SVD situation report / Africa CDC weekly update. YES iff the DRC cumulative confirmed-death count reported in a source dated on/after 2026-07-05 is >= 700; otherwise NO. Only figures published on/after the 2026-07-05 lower bound count; the pre-registration 452 baseline cannot itself resolve this.

public_question: Will the DRC terminal 7-day confirmed-death velocity (new confirmed deaths per day, averaged over the most recent 7-day reporting window) fall below 15 deaths/day at the 2026-08-04 resolution, versus a frozen registration baseline of approximately 18 deaths/day as of 2026-07-05? Resolves on the latest WHO/Africa CDC cumulative-death figures dated on or after 2026-07-05, differenced across their two most recent reports spanning roughly 7 days.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Source: successive WHO DON / WHO AFRO SVD sitreps / Africa CDC updates dated on/after 2026-07-05. Compute terminal velocity as (latest cumulative confirmed deaths minus the cumulative confirmed deaths reported ~7 days earlier) divided by the number of days between those two on/after-2026-07-05 reports. YES (down) iff that terminal per-day rate < 15; NO iff >= 15. Both endpoints must be dated on/after the 2026-07-05 lower bound.

public_question: Will the DRC delay-adjusted case-fatality ratio (confirmed deaths divided by confirmed cases lagged to account for reporting delay) remain at or above 38% at the 2026-08-04 resolution, versus a frozen registration baseline of a crude CFR of ~31% (452/1460) and a delay-adjusted CFR of ~43% as of 2026-07-05? Resolves on the latest WHO/Africa CDC confirmed case and death totals dated on or after 2026-07-05.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Source: WHO DON / WHO AFRO SVD sitrep / Africa CDC update dated on/after 2026-07-05. Delay-adjusted CFR = cumulative confirmed deaths / cumulative confirmed cases as of a report ~10-14 days earlier (published on/after 2026-07-05 where available); where a delay-adjusted figure is not separately published, use the crude cumulative CFR from the latest on/after-2026-07-05 report as a floor. YES (hold-high) iff the delay-adjusted CFR >= 38%; NO iff < 38%. Only on/after-2026-07-05 figures count.

public_question: Will cumulative confirmed BDBV deaths in North Kivu province, DRC reach 80 or more, as reported in a WHO or Africa CDC province-disaggregated update dated on or after 2026-07-05? Frozen registration baseline is 71 confirmed North Kivu deaths (WHO DON612, 2026-07-01). Resolves YES only if a governing public source dated on/after 2026-07-05 reports a cumulative North Kivu confirmed-death figure of at least 80.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Source: WHO DON / WHO AFRO SVD sitrep / Africa CDC update carrying province-level North Kivu disaggregation, dated on/after 2026-07-05. YES iff cumulative North Kivu confirmed deaths reported on/after 2026-07-05 >= 80; otherwise NO. The pre-registration 71 baseline cannot resolve this; requires a fresh on/after-2026-07-05 province figure.

public_question: Over the registration-to-resolution window, will North Kivu's share of NEW confirmed DRC deaths (North Kivu death increment divided by national death increment, both measured between the first on/after-2026-07-05 report and the resolution report) stay at or below North Kivu's frozen stock share of ~15.7% (71 of 452 national deaths as of 2026-07-05)? Resolves on province-disaggregated WHO/Africa CDC death figures dated on or after 2026-07-05.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Source: two province-disaggregated WHO/Africa CDC reports dated on/after 2026-07-05. New-death share = (North Kivu deaths at resolution minus North Kivu deaths at the first on/after-2026-07-05 report) / (national deaths at resolution minus national deaths at that same first report). YES (hold, not rising) iff that incremental share <= 15.7%; NO iff > 15.7%. If the national increment is zero, the pin is unscoreable on the share and resolves on whether North Kivu's absolute death count rose (rose = NO, held = YES). Both endpoints on/after the 2026-07-05 lower bound.

public_question: Will the crude case-fatality-ratio gap between North Kivu and Ituri provinces, DRC narrow (get smaller in percentage points) by the 2026-08-04 resolution, versus a frozen registration baseline gap of ~28.8 points (North Kivu ~57.3% CFR 71/124, minus Ituri ~28.5% CFR 380/1333, as of 2026-07-05)? Resolves on province-disaggregated confirmed case and death figures from WHO/Africa CDC dated on or after 2026-07-05.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Source: WHO/Africa CDC province-disaggregated report dated on/after 2026-07-05. Compute North Kivu crude CFR (NK deaths/NK cases) and Ituri crude CFR (Ituri deaths/Ituri cases) from the latest on/after-2026-07-05 figures; gap = NK CFR minus Ituri CFR in percentage points. YES (narrows) iff the resolution gap < 28.8 points; NO iff >= 28.8 points. Only on/after-2026-07-05 province figures count; the 28.8-point baseline is frozen from the pre-registration snapshot for comparison only.

public_question: Will the South Kivu province, DRC front escalate on EITHER measure, as reported by WHO/Africa CDC in an update dated on or after 2026-07-05: cumulative confirmed South Kivu cases reach 6 or more (at least double the frozen baseline of 3), OR cumulative confirmed South Kivu deaths reach 5 or more (up from the frozen baseline of 1)? Frozen registration baseline is 3 confirmed cases and 1 death in South Kivu (1 affected health zone) as of 2026-07-05. Resolves YES if either arm is met by a source dated on/after 2026-07-05.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Source: WHO DON / WHO AFRO SVD sitrep / Africa CDC update with South Kivu disaggregation, dated on/after 2026-07-05. YES iff EITHER cumulative confirmed South Kivu cases >= 6 OR cumulative confirmed South Kivu deaths >= 5, as reported on/after 2026-07-05; NO iff neither arm is met. The pre-registration 3-cases/1-death baseline cannot resolve either arm; a fresh on/after-2026-07-05 figure is required.

public_question: Forecast: NO onward EU/EEA case will occur that is epidemiologically linked to the France index case (the ALIMA physician confirmed 24 June 2026 on return from Ituri), where 'onward' means a NEW confirmed BDBV case in any EU/EEA country whose infection is attributed by ECDC/WHO to transmission from the France index case (secondary or contact case), with detection/confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05. Frozen baseline at registration: France had 1 confirmed case with NO onward transmission and ECDC assessed EU/EEA risk as very low. Resolves YES (containment holds) if no such linked EU/EEA onward case is reported by 2026-08-04; resolves NO if any such linked case is reported.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: ECDC communicable disease threats / rapid risk assessment updates or WHO Disease Outbreak News. YES if, on evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05 and by 2026-08-04, no EU/EEA case epidemiologically linked (secondary/contact) to the France index case is reported. NO if any such onward EU/EEA case is confirmed. The 24-June France index case itself does not count (predates the 2026-07-05 lower bound and is not onward). Lower bound: only evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05.

public_question: Forecast: France remains at 1 confirmed BDBV case with NO second confirmed case (cumulative France confirmed count stays <=1). Frozen baseline at registration: France = 1 confirmed (the ALIMA physician confirmed 24 June 2026), no second case. Resolves YES if France's cumulative confirmed BDBV count is still exactly 1 as of 2026-08-04 (i.e., no additional French confirmation is reported on evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05); resolves NO if a second (or further) confirmed BDBV case in France is reported on or after 2026-07-05.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: WHO Disease Outbreak News or ECDC updates reporting the France cumulative confirmed count. YES if the France confirmed count remains 1 through 2026-08-04. NO if any additional France confirmation (count >=2) is reported dated on or after 2026-07-05. The 24-June index case is the frozen baseline of 1 and does not by itself trigger NO. Lower bound: only evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05.

public_question: Forecast (directional expansion signal): a genuinely NEW country beyond the current affected set {DRC, Uganda, France, United States} IS added to the WHO/IHR-recognized affected-country list for the BDBV-2026 outbreak. Frozen baseline at registration: affected countries = {DRC, Uganda, France, United States} (4). NOTE: Germany is explicitly NOT a new country here (the case medically evacuated to Germany is a US citizen, i.e. counted under United States); a US-citizen medevac to any country does not create a new affected country. Resolves YES if WHO (Disease Outbreak News/IHR) recognizes at least one country NOT in that set as affected (a confirmed BDBV case attributed to this outbreak), on evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05 and by 2026-08-04; resolves NO if the recognized affected set remains {DRC, Uganda, France, United States}.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: WHO Disease Outbreak News / IHR affected-country reporting. YES if WHO recognizes >=1 country outside {DRC, Uganda, France, United States} as having a confirmed BDBV-2026 case, dated on or after 2026-07-05. A medical evacuation of a US citizen (e.g. to Germany) does NOT count as a new country. NO if the affected set is unchanged. Complement of INTL-FEED-GRAIN. Lower bound: only evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05.

public_question: Forecast (base-rate / stability control): WHO adds NO country beyond the current affected set {DRC, Uganda, France, United States}; the internationally recognized affected-country list stays exactly these four. Frozen baseline at registration: 4 affected countries {DRC, Uganda, France, United States}. Resolves YES (no expansion) if, on WHO evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05 and through 2026-08-04, no country outside that set is recognized as affected; resolves NO if any new country is added. A US-citizen medical evacuation (e.g. to Germany) does NOT count as adding a country.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: WHO Disease Outbreak News / IHR affected-country reporting. YES if the recognized affected set remains exactly {DRC, Uganda, France, United States} through 2026-08-04; NO if WHO recognizes any additional affected country dated on or after 2026-07-05. Directional complement of P9 (P9 YES <=> this NO). Medevac of a US citizen to another country is not a new affected country. Lower bound: only evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), no public source documents a routine alert-to-lab-confirmation turnaround of under 7 days for the DRC BDBV response: WHO added the first BDBV diagnostic to Emergency Use Listing on 2 July 2026, but it is a LAB-BASED molecular (PCR) assay, not point-of-care, and post-mortem sampling reticence continues to delay confirmation. FORECAST (YES): the alert-to-lab-confirmation latency stays at or above 7 days, i.e. no public source dated on or after 2026-07-05 documents a sustained sub-7-day median alert-to-confirmation turnaround for the DRC response. Resolves NO only if such a sub-7-day turnaround is publicly documented after 7/5.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: WHO Disease Outbreak News / AFRO SitRep / INSP RDC SitRep / ECDC dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = no dated-on/after-7/5 public report states a routine or median alert-to-lab-confirmation turnaround below 7 days (the lab-based EUL PCR does not change field turnaround absent explicit reporting). NO = a public source dated on/after 2026-07-05 documents a sustained sub-7-day median. Lower bound: only evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05 counts.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), the DRC BDBV surveillance signal chain runs detection-then-confirmation: community alerts and suspected/probable cases are logged in the SitRep before lab confirmation lands, so for at least one health zone the first public detection signal (alert, suspected case, or newly-affected-zone flag) precedes its lab-confirmed count. FORECAST (YES): this detection-precedes-confirmation ordering persists for at least one zone in a public SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05, i.e. at least one newly-flagged or extending zone shows a public detection/alert or suspected signal ahead of (or without) same-report lab confirmation. Resolves NO only if every zone in the first on/after-7/5 SitRep shows confirmation without any prior public detection lead.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: INSP RDC SitRep / WHO AFRO SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = at least one zone in a SitRep dated on/after 7/5 exhibits a detection/alert/suspected signal preceding or without same-report lab confirmation (the standing pattern). NO = no such zone in the first on/after-7/5 SitRep. Lower bound: only SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05 count.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), NO BDBV-specific point-of-care or rapid antigen assay is publicly documented as deployed in the field: the 2 July 2026 WHO Emergency Use Listing covers a LAB-BASED molecular (PCR) test, and point-of-care/rapid assays remain under evaluation, not deployed. FORECAST (YES = hold): no public source dated on or after 2026-07-05 documents a BDBV-specific point-of-care or rapid antigen assay as DEPLOYED in field use in DRC or Uganda. Resolves NO if such a field deployment is publicly documented after 7/5.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: WHO EUL / WHO DON / AFRO SitRep / FIND / manufacturer or partner report dated on or after 2026-07-05. NO (the pin flips) = a public source dated on/after 7/5 documents a BDBV-specific point-of-care or rapid antigen assay actually deployed/in field use (not merely EUL-listed, not merely under evaluation). YES = no such deployment documented. The lab-based EUL PCR of 2 July does NOT satisfy the deployment condition. Lower bound: only evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05 counts.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), the LOVS public latency/evidence observatory (the public Arcede/LOVS BDBV surface and its published change ledger) stands at its 2026-07-05 published state. FORECAST (YES): the public observatory gains at least one NEW public-bytes edition after registration, i.e. a public surface-change-log or snapshot edition dated on or after 2026-07-05 is published extending the latency/detection evidence record beyond the 7/5 baseline. Resolves NO if no new public edition is published on or after 7/5 within the horizon.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: the public LOVS surface change log / published snapshot ledger (arcede.com BDBV surface) dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = at least one new public-bytes edition (surface change-log entry or snapshot edition) published on/after 7/5 and on/before the resolution date 2026-08-04. NO = no new public edition in that window. Lower bound: only editions dated on or after 2026-07-05 count.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), the ratio of new confirmed cases to samples tested (lab positivity) in the DRC BDBV response is running in the high-teens-to-low-30s percent (SitRep-47, 2026-06-30: lab positivity 29.9%, samples tested 244 that period; recent prior periods 17.7%-18.4%) while per-period samples tested has RISEN materially from the ~35-70 range of late May to the 140-265 range of late June. FORECAST (YES = plateau, not hidden growth): across public SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05, lab positivity (new confirmed / samples tested) FALLS or stays roughly FLAT while samples tested per period holds or rises, i.e. the response is widening the net rather than a rising positivity revealing hidden growth. Resolves NO if positivity rises materially and sustainedly WHILE testing also rises (the hidden-growth signature).
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: INSP RDC / WHO AFRO SitRep operational-indicator series (lab_positivity_rate and samples_tested) dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = over the on/after-7/5 SitReps, mean lab positivity does not rise materially above the ~29.9% 7/5 anchor while mean samples-tested-per-period holds at or above the ~200 late-June level (positivity flat/down as testing holds/rises = widening net). NO = positivity climbs materially and is sustained while testing also climbs (hidden-growth signature). NEVER infer incidence growth from alert or case volume alone. Lower bound: only SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05 count.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), the national DRC contact-tracing follow-up (coverage) rate stands at 82.5% (SitRep-47, 2026-06-30; Ituri 82.6%, Nord-Kivu 81.9%), below the 95% interruption target and down from 87.1% at SitRep-44. FORECAST (YES = holds): the national contact-tracing follow-up rate HOLDS at or above 80% in public SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05, i.e. it does not degrade below 80%. Resolves NO if the national follow-up rate falls below 80% in a public SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: INSP RDC / WHO AFRO SitRep contact_followup_rate (national) dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = the latest national contact-tracing follow-up rate in a SitRep dated on/after 7/5 is >=80%. NO = it falls below 80%. Frozen baseline 82.5% (2026-06-30). Lower bound: only SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05 count; contact-followup reporting incompleteness is not read as proof contacts were unmonitored.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), ETU/isolation-bed occupancy strain is high: national isolation occupancy 92.7% and Nord-Kivu CTE saturation 138.8% (over capacity) per the SitRep-47 (2026-06-30) challenge table. FORECAST (YES = strain persists/rises): in public SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05, national isolation occupancy stays at or above 85%, OR at least one province (e.g. Nord-Kivu) remains over 100% saturation, i.e. bed-occupancy strain does not ease below those thresholds. Resolves NO if national isolation occupancy falls below 85% AND no province remains over 100% saturation in a public SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: INSP RDC / WHO AFRO SitRep isolation-occupancy / CTE-saturation challenge table dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = latest national isolation occupancy >=85% OR any province CTE saturation >100% in a SitRep dated on/after 7/5. NO = national <85% AND no province >100%. Frozen baseline: national 92.7%, Nord-Kivu 138.8% (2026-06-30). Lower bound: only SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05 count.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), onset-to-isolation delay in the DRC BDBV response remains long relative to the sub-3-day early-isolation goal: Nord-Kivu shows the highest provincial case-fatality (59.2%) with CTE saturation at 138.8%, and Mongbwalu carries a 47.7% zone case-fatality consistent with late presentation, indicating persistent delay between symptom onset and isolation. FORECAST (YES = shortens): a public source dated on or after 2026-07-05 documents onset-to-isolation (or admission) delay SHORTENING versus the registration baseline, i.e. a measurable reduction in mean/median onset-to-isolation days. Resolves NO if no public source dated on or after 2026-07-05 documents a shortening (delay holds or lengthens).
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: INSP RDC / WHO AFRO / partner report documenting onset-to-isolation or onset-to-admission delay, dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = a dated-on/after-7/5 public source reports a shortened onset-to-isolation delay vs baseline. NO = no such shortening documented (delay holds/lengthens, the standing signal). Baseline delay is long (proxied by high late-presentation CFR: Nord-Kivu 59.2%, Mongbwalu 47.7% at 2026-06-30). Lower bound: only evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05 counts.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), ring vaccination for the BDBV outbreak is publicly documented in the established response footprint. FORECAST (YES = expands): a public source dated on or after 2026-07-05 documents ring vaccination being INITIATED or EXPANDED into a NEW health zone or district not previously reported as vaccinated (an extension of the ring-vaccination footprint beyond the 2026-07-05 baseline set). Resolves NO if no public source dated on or after 2026-07-05 documents ring vaccination initiated or expanded in a new zone.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: WHO DON / AFRO SitRep / INSP RDC / Uganda MoH / GAVI dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = a dated-on/after-7/5 public source documents ring vaccination newly initiated or expanded to a zone/district not previously covered. NO = no such new-zone expansion documented in the window. Forward pin: only a NEW zone beyond the baseline footprint counts. Lower bound: only evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05 counts.

public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), Rwanda has NO confirmed BDBV case, despite Karisimbi (North Kivu) sitting ~9 km from the nearest DRC edge to Rwanda. FORECAST (YES = zero-hold holds): Rwanda records NO first lab-confirmed BDBV case dated on or after 2026-07-05, i.e. the Rwanda border-district zero-hold persists through the resolution date. Resolves NO if Rwanda's first lab-confirmed BDBV case is dated on or after 2026-07-05.
resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.
notes: Resolution source: WHO Disease Outbreak News / AFRO / ECDC / Rwanda Biomedical Centre dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = no Rwanda BDBV case with first lab-confirmation dated on/after 7/5 through 2026-08-04. NO = Rwanda's first lab-confirmed BDBV case is dated on/after 7/5. Rwanda is NOT a currently-affected country, so any confirmation dated on/after 7/5 flips the pin (no look-ahead risk). Lower bound: only first-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05 counts.
